_meta.keyKnowledge: We practise calming strategies with a trusted person when our feelings are big.

_source.w: We practise calming strategies with a trusted person when our feelings are big.
y: Learning to use calming strategies helps us manage strong emotions.
Practising with a trusted person builds our ability to self-regulate over time.
These strategies help us feel in control when emotions feel overwhelming.
eg: We take deep breaths with an adult when we feel upset.
We use a calm-down corner or quiet space when we need it.
We hold a comfort object when we feel worried.
We count slowly with an adult to help settle big feelings.
We listen to calming music or sounds together.
We use a visual emotions chart to identify and manage our feelings.
